question: Which age groups spend the most?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN u.age < 25 THEN '18-24' WHEN u.age < 35 THEN '25-34' WHEN u.age < 45 THEN '35-44' WHEN u.age < 55 THEN '45-54' ELSE '55+' END AS age_group, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price) / COUNT(DISTINCT u.id), 2) AS spend_per_user FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` u ON u.id = oi.user_id WHERE oi.status IN ('Complete', 'Shipped') GROUP BY age_group ORDER BY age_group
analyst_notes: Bucket ages into standard bands; raw ages are noise. Show BOTH total revenue and per-user spend — bands have different populations, so totals alone mislead (same normalization rule as state comparisons).
author: claude-draft
